tokens: Are you getting any picture, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are you getting any picture, yet?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I didn't hear what you said.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That AGC is a great idea.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, I'm ready to go to work.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, we want you to try to get the heat flow drill in at least another section.
We think that perhaps there might be an extra section added onto the unit you started yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: If you'll tell me what's there, I'll continue.
If one or maybe two sections, however many you think you can put on and still drill.
Once you get the sections on, we want you to use the drill again.
And first, recycle the chuck several times, as you used to do in the simulations, and then start the drill, and put only a few pounds of force on top of the drill.
And, while it's running, if you notice from the torque that it starts to seize up, we want you to try to pull it back out of the hole a bit to free it, as you drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Roger, just try to free the torque that the drill is picking up from the soil.
It seems to be binding around it.
Soil and/or rock, that's binding around it.
And we think that the secret to this...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...may be not to put quite so much pressure on the top of the drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...you might want to (garbled) before you get started.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Jim, how are you doing?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We want you to deploy the flag after you finish the photography.
And, we're wondering at the moment where the two empty core tubes are.
If they are still in bag 5.
we'll want you to carry them in your hand out toward the ALSEP station later on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, they are under my seat, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, and unless I miss my guess, your seat's out near the ALSEP now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Why do you want them out near the ALSEP, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...know how to break this news to you, but we're going to do Station 8 out at the ALSEP site, or nearby.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, Joe, before we got out this morning, we figured you guys had a conspiracy against us, having Jim doing Station 8 and me drilling at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It may work out that way.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Jim, you just could get the flag out; don't necessarily deploy it.
We'll get that later when the TV's back at Falcon.
And keep me posted on your progress...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: When you finish pulling the flag out of its holder, but not yet deployed, we'll ask you to walk on out to the ALSEP site.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Joe, I've got the drill on one extra section now.
Run through it again, please, just so I don't (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We are interested in your starting to drill.
We've got a lot of power left in the drill, just run it around several times; and don't bear down on it too much.
Let's see how free - (correcting himself) or how freely - it moves in the surficial layer there, first of all.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Joe, I put very little force on it, and it binds up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Any luck by trying to pull it back a bit out of the hole to free it?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Chuckles) No, it pulls me right on down with it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, stand by a second.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, is it possible at all to clear out the flutes on it by lifting up as you turn the power on?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But it seems to want to pull me with it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I have the flag unpacked here, but not deployed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We want you now to proceed on out toward Dave, and be back with you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You want me to carry the staff and the flag out there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Negative, negative, leave it near the LM.
We'll pick it up a little later when we come back to the Falcon.
Just leave it there in a convenient place.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Chuckles) I thought I was going to be able do the Station 8 with the flag waving in the background.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now I've got the drill partially out.
Do you want me to try and take the drill off the probe (means the stem)?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we wonder if you can just hold it there.
Begin it running, and ease it back down into the hole, but without a whole lot of force, down into the hole.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, just let it run for a while.
There's a lot of power in that battery.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I'm not...(Hears Joe) I'm not putting any force on it.
I'm letting it do its own forcing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We've got a lot of power to burn.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I want to come out and get some of that!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Grunting as he pulls the drill a few inches upward) It starts to bind up every once in a while.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When you get out to the ALSEP site, once again being very careful with your dust, and particularly the exposed SIDE experiment, we'd like for you to do a photo pan out there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Dave, take a breather there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah; (chuckling) it's tightening up again, Joe, and I'm not putting any force on it all.
It pulls itself down in; and then it starts to bind up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, I hope we get a chance to pick that rock up before we go back.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, is it a nice one?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...as you can tell, that drill is going down.
We're going to ask for about 2 more minutes and call it quits, probably.
But just take a breather there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I just don't want to break it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I'm at the ALSEP site, and I'm tippy-toeing over to the LSM (Lunar Surface Magnetometer).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Gee, Joe, I think I got through something.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me take a little break here.
It just started easing up there and went down a little easier.
Like (laughing) we might have got through a layer, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Joking) I hope we're not going to let the air out (of the Moon).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, I'm wondering, if we're really serious about Station 8, whether maybe we could get started on that, and I could be doing...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You ought to do that if you're really going to do Station 8.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That sounds good to us, Jim.
And Dave, I think maybe you could put another section on that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's see if I can get the drill off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, you had a question about the bubble on the Central Station?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, we're happy with that; no problem.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; because the outer edge of the bubble is at the outer edge of the black mark.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, you've got your camera there...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...you might go out and start to photograph the flow site, if that looks like a reasonable thing.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave didn't get those pictures yesterday, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I didn't get the heat flow, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...have you taken a photo pan from the ALSEP site?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm on my way (to get the heat flow photos).
(Responding to Joe's question about the pan) No.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll probably be running out of film.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll have to go back and change mags.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...under the seat there, if you want to change them out.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, the pan at the ALSEP site's complete.
I'll go out and photo the heat flow.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You've got just one minute of drilling left.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The only things that give out are the hands.
I'll tell you...You know, all this working with the gloves on, after a while...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, this mag ran out.
I'm going to go back and change.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, we're satisfied with this drill hole.
Suggest you stop, pull the drill off, and emplace the heat flow probe.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We made a little money, didn't we?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Joe) I don't think we have another color mag out here, do we, Joe?
We'll have to use black and white...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That is to say, any mag that's empty.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: That is, any mag that's still full and not exposed, and you can look at the frame number.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I thought you'd be able to give me a quick reading.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Somehow, all this wire down close to the probes got wrapped with Teflon or something - makes it a lot bigger than we've been used to seeing (in training).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Can always pull those out and put four more in.
(Pause) Joe, I don't think I got the probes all way down.
I think that probably one of those cores may have been bent or something.
What do you think about that?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, would it be possible to pull it up a little bit so you can see the top of the probe and then put the rammer-jammer right on top of it?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: That's where I had it, Joe.
The probe went down a couple, two out of the four.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, pull the probe out all the way, and see if the rammer-jammer alone will go in, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And the front of the probe (meaning the tip that goes into the hole first) looks okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't we try it again?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Of course the tip of the rammer-jammer is smaller than the tip of the probe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, the ALSEP pictures are complete.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's it, Joe, it won't go further unless I try and force it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...we've decided it's about time you start on your Station 8 trench, if you would, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Resting while he talks) Hey, Joe, it won't go in any further than that without really trying to force it.
I could try and push it with the rammer if you want, but I suspect the first thing (chuckles) would be the rammer would collapse.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, our reading is: using your calibrated arm, put about 15 to 20 pounds of force on it, and we'll be satisfied with whatever we get.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Resting as he talks) It's stuck, Joe, and I think where it's stuck is where the third probe joins the second probe.
And, you know, you can never get those things apart.
But I got four other probes in here; if you want me to pull out the four we got and stick the other four in, in hopes that it gets down some distance, we could try that if you like.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Dave, we'd like the rammer-jammer reading and have it pulled out, then we'll take what we got.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Sorry I couldn't get it all the way in, because that sure isn't very far.
(Reading a mark on the rammer where it goes into the top drill stem) Bravo 9.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Never mind, we got it already off the TV.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Of just how high the pipe comes up above the surface.
We'd like for you to make sure the dirt is solid against the outside of the pipe, and then ask you to police the area as best you can of foreign objects.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The other thing I'm going to do, Joe, is re-align the...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...leave, we want you to make sure the cables are lying as flatly as possible on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I'll also make sure that the box (meaning the Heat Flow Electronics package or HFE) is aligned.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I'll leave this here; maybe the next guy can fix it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, you'll be interested to know that probe went in as far as it will go.
That's as deep as the hole was.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Dave, and while you are there at the box, could you check to see if the Boyd bolt cup - the sleeves - have been taken away from the box.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, there were a couple of red washers sitting on the connectors there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, how are you doing?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I picked up a pink rock and a black rock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm just resting up for Station 8.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (As) soon at the boss (meaning Dave) tells me where...Tell me where the boss wants it dug.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Joe, (the HFE is) aligned and the number is...(The gnomon shadow is) just about down to the centerline on 2.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, what are you up to there?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I'm just checking the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Joe, if Jim took...(To Jim) Hey, don't touch it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Joe, if Jim took a picture of the heat flow box, the one he took probably isn't representative of the proper alignment, which it now has.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll come over and take another one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, just make sure you are well clear of the (Central Station) antenna, with your PLSS there, old friend.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I need an antenna like that on my PLSS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I picked up that black, glassy rock, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And I picked up another pink one that looked like it had a lot of the plagioclase glass in it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Don't step on this (cable between the HFE and the east probe); I'm trying to get it stretched out flat.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's got some dirt on it, but that's all right.
transcript.speaker: Scott
